Sentences used:
(1) Birds fly and eat.
(2) John is a baby bird.
(3) John does not fly?
Knowledge used:
If some X is a baby bird then X is a bird. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
Proof steps:
(1) If some X is a baby bird then X is a bird  [background knowledge]
(2) John is a baby bird  [sentence 2]
(3) John is a bird  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If some X is a bird then normally the flying event sk4 of X is a capability  [sentence 1]
(5) Normally the flying event sk4 of John 1 is a capability  [from steps 3, 4]
(6) If some X is a bird then X performs the flying event sk4 of X  [sentence 1]
(7) John performs the flying event sk4 of John 1  [from steps 3, 6]
(8) If answer holds and X is a fly event and John performs X and X is an activity then X is not a capability  [sentence 3]
(9) Answer holds  [assumption]
(10) If the flying event sk4 of John 1 is an activity and sk4 of John 1 is a fly event then sk4 of John 1 is not a capability  [from steps 7, 8, 9]
(11) If some X is a bird then sk4 of X is a fly event  [sentence 1]
(12) sk4 of John 1 is a fly event  [from steps 3, 11]
(13) If some X is a bird then the flying event sk4 of X is an activity  [sentence 1]
(14) The flying event sk4 of John 1 is an activity  [from steps 3, 13]
(15) sk4 of John 1 is not a capability  [from steps 10, 12, 14]
